apiKeys.updateFailedMessage: The key status change did not finish. Retry before relying on the new state.
apiKeys.deleteFailedTitle: API key could not be deleted
apiKeys.deleteFailedMessage: The delete request did not finish. The key may still be active.

deleteDialog.detailRecovery: Create a new key if an integration needs access again.

apiKeys.copyFailedTitle: API key could not be copied

docs.title: API Documentation
docs.intro: Use PDF-Flow's REST API for document workflows that need backend processing, async status tracking, and auditable enterprise access.
docs.authDescription: Create API keys from the Enterprise dashboard and send them in the Authorization header. Full keys are shown once, while audit and usage remain backend-controlled.
docs.uploadDescription: Upload a source PDF or supported file and receive a server-side file reference for processing.
docs.mergeDescription: Create an async merge job from uploaded file references and return a job id.
docs.splitDescription: Create an async split job using explicit page ranges or extraction rules.
docs.ocrDescription: Run cloud OCR on PDFs or images when local extraction is not enough.
docs.jobStatusDescription: Poll job status, progress, diagnostic code, and result metadata.
docs.downloadDescription: Download the generated file after the backend marks the job complete.
docs.workflowUpload: Upload files and keep the returned file references in your workflow state.
docs.workflowProcess: Start a processing endpoint; PDF-Flow returns a job id instead of blocking the request.
docs.workflowNotify: Poll the job endpoint or subscribe to webhook events for completion and failure states.
docs.workflowDownload: Download the result only after the backend reports a completed job.
docs.webhooksDescription: Subscribe endpoints to operational events so automations can react without aggressive polling.
docs.signatureDescription: Webhook secrets are verified with HMAC-SHA256 signatures. Reject unsigned or mismatched events in your integration.
docs.rateLimitsDescription: API rate limits depend on your API key configuration.
docs.rateLimitDefault: Default keys can be limited per hour, or set to unlimited for trusted internal workers.
docs.rateLimitCustom: Use separate keys for production, staging, and batch jobs so incidents can be isolated.
docs.rateLimitHeaders: Responses include rate limit context when throttling or quota checks apply.
docs.paymentBoundaryDescription: Plan, quota, and payment entitlement are never inferred from frontend return pages. The backend verifies provider callbacks and account state before API access or quota changes are trusted.
docs.errorHandlingDescription: Errors use JSON with stable codes and a request id so support can trace failures without asking for document contents.
